name: verify
context: Verify phone numbers and email addresses using Twilio Verify (OTP, push, TOTP)
info: This skill covers phone number and email verification using the Twilio Verify API. It handles sending one-time passcodes (OTP) via SMS, voice, email, or WhatsApp, and checking the code the user submits. Verify manages code generation, delivery, expiration, rate limiting, and fraud detection — you do not need to build these yourself. The two-step flow is: (1) create a Verification to send the code, (2) create a Verification Check to validate the user's input. Verify also supports push notifications and TOTP (authenticator apps) for advanced 2FA use cases.
body:
# Phone and Email Verification

Verify phone numbers and email addresses using the Twilio Verify API. Verify handles the entire OTP lifecycle: code generation, delivery, expiration, rate limiting, and fraud detection.

## Sending a Verification Code

```javascript
const twilio = require("twilio");

const client = twilio(
  process.env.TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID,
  process.env.TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN
);

const verification = await client.verify.v2
  .services(process.env.TWILIO_VERIFY_SERVICE_SID)
  .verifications.create({
    to: "+15551234567",
    channel: "sms", // "sms", "call", "email", "whatsapp"
  });

console.log(verification.sid); // VE...
console.log(verification.status); // "pending"
```

## Checking the Code

```javascript
const verificationCheck = await client.verify.v2
  .services(process.env.TWILIO_VERIFY_SERVICE_SID)
  .verificationChecks.create({
    to: "+15551234567",
    code: "123456", // Code entered by the user
  });

console.log(verificationCheck.status); // "approved" or "pending"
console.log(verificationCheck.valid); // true or false
```

## Channels

Verify supports multiple delivery channels:

| Channel | Value | Description |
|---------|-------|-------------|
| SMS | `sms` | 6-digit code via text message |
| Voice | `call` | Code read aloud via phone call |
| Email | `email` | Code sent to email address (requires SendGrid integration) |
| WhatsApp | `whatsapp` | Code sent via WhatsApp message (see [[twilio/whatsapp]]) |
| Push | `push` | Push notification to a registered device (requires Verify Push SDK) |
| TOTP | `totp` | Time-based one-time password via authenticator app |

## Rate Limits

Verify enforces built-in rate limits to prevent abuse:

- **5 verification attempts** per phone number per service per 10 minutes
- **5 check attempts** per verification (locks after 5 failed checks)
- Verifications expire after **10 minutes** by default

Custom rate limits can be configured per service for stricter control.

## Verify Service Setup

Create a Verify Service in the Twilio Console (Verify > Services) or via the API:

```javascript
const service = await client.verify.v2.services.create({
  friendlyName: "My App Verification",
  codeLength: 6,
});

console.log(service.sid); // VA...
// Store this as TWILIO_VERIFY_SERVICE_SID
```

The service SID (starts with `VA`) is used for all verification operations. Configure code length, delivery channels, and branding in the service settings.

sections.title: Send verification code
sections.body: Use `client.verify.v2.services(serviceSid).verifications.create({ to, channel })` to send a code. The `to` field is a phone number (E.164) for SMS/call/WhatsApp or an email address for the email channel. The `channel` selects the delivery method. Response includes `verificationSid` and `status` (`pending`). See [[twilio#authentication]] for credential setup.

sections.title: Check verification code
sections.body: Use `client.verify.v2.services(serviceSid).verificationChecks.create({ to, code })` to validate the user's input. If the code matches, `status` is `approved` and `valid` is `true`. If incorrect, `status` remains `pending` and `valid` is `false`. After 5 failed attempts, the verification is locked and a new one must be sent.

sections.title: Delivery channels
sections.body: SMS is the default and most common channel. Voice reads the code aloud (useful as fallback — see [[twilio/voice]]). Email requires a SendGrid integration configured on the Verify Service. WhatsApp delivers via the WhatsApp Business API (see [[twilio/whatsapp]]). Push requires the Verify Push SDK in the mobile app. TOTP works with standard authenticator apps (Google Authenticator, Authy).

sections.title: Rate limits and security
sections.body: Built-in rate limits: 5 sends per number per 10 minutes, 5 check attempts per verification, 10-minute expiration. Custom rate limits can be configured per service. Verify also includes fraud detection via Fraud Guard, which blocks high-risk verifications to known fraud destinations. Enable Fraud Guard in the Verify Service settings.

scripts.name: Send Verification
scripts.description: Send a verification code to a phone number or email using Twilio Verify

to.description: Phone number (E.164) or email address to verify

channel.description: Delivery channel for the verification code

verificationSid.description: Unique identifier for the verification (starts with VE)

valid.description: Whether the submitted code was valid (on check)

depends.reason: Requires Twilio account credentials and a Verify Service

links.context: SMS is the primary delivery channel for verification codes
Voice channel reads the code aloud as a fallback
Account credentials for Verify API access

related.note: Verify uses SMS as its primary delivery channel
Voice is a common fallback channel for OTP delivery
WhatsApp provides an alternative OTP delivery channel
